meta.name: Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)
meta.subtitle: Charred yogurt-marinated chicken in a silky tomato-butter sauce

source.name: SSA canonical cuisines/indian/recipes/butter_chicken.json
notes: Canonical butter chicken baseline with deterministic adaptation hooks (oven/broiler/grill/stovetop char).
Sauce is smooth and balanced: tomato + butter + cream, finished with fenugreek (kasuri methi).
Includes yogurt curdle guard + optional dairy-free variants.

summary: Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) made the SSA way: yogurt-marinated, charred chicken folded into a silky tomato-butter sauce with cream and kasuri methi. Balanced, mildly spiced, and perfect with basmati rice or naan.

yield.notes: Serves 6 with rice/naan; serves 8 with multiple sides.

optional.label: Grill
optional.notes: Great for char. Use in place of oven/broiler.

optional.label: Air fryer
optional.notes: Fast char option; cook in batches.

signals.freezerFriendly: yes (freeze sauce + chicken; finish with kasuri methi after reheating)

notes: Contains dairy: yogurt, butter, cream (unless using dairy-free variation).
Nut-free by default (many restaurant versions use cashew); optional cashew is listed as a variation.

ingredients.label: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs
notes: Thighs stay juicy under high heat.
You can use chicken breast; reduce cook time and avoid overcooking.

ingredients.label: Plain yogurt (full-fat preferred)
notes: Full-fat yogurt is more stable and less likely to curdle.
For dairy-free: use coconut yogurt (unsweetened).

ingredients.label: Lemon juice
ingredients.notes: In the marinade; optional extra squeeze at the end for brightness.

ingredients.label: Kashmiri chili powder
ingredients.notes: Color + mild warmth. Substitute mild chili powder + a pinch of paprika.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Use 1.5 tsp in the marinade; reserve the rest for sauce adjustment.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: Classic makhani richness. Add in stages to control separation.

ingredients.label: Crushed tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Or blend ripe tomatoes for a fresher version.

ingredients.label: Garam masala
ingredients.notes: Add at the end for aroma.

ingredients.label: Kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves)
ingredients.notes: Crush between fingers before adding. Signature butter chicken aroma.

ingredients.label: Heavy cream
notes: For lighter: use half-and-half (sauce may be thinner).
For dairy-free: use coconut cream; taste and adjust acid/salt.

steps.title: Marinate the chicken
instructions: In a bowl, mix yogurt, lemon juice, garlic, ginger, Kashmiri chili, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and 1.5 tsp of the salt.
Add chicken and coat thoroughly.
Marinate 20–30 minutes (or up to overnight, covered, refrigerated).
checks: If marinating overnight, keep salt as listed (helps flavor); do not exceed 24 hours.

steps.title: Char the chicken (broiler/oven/grill/air fryer)
instructions: Heat broiler to high OR oven to 500°F (260°C). Line a sheet pan with foil.
Spread chicken in a single layer; drizzle with 1 tbsp oil if it looks dry.
Broil 8–12 minutes, flipping once, until browned/charred at edges and cooked through (165°F / 74°C).
Alternative: Grill over high heat 3–4 minutes per side; or air fry at 400°F for 10–14 minutes, shaking halfway.
checks: You want browned edges for smoky flavor.
Do not overcook breast meat—pull at 160°F and rest to carryover to 165°F.

steps.title: Build the makhani sauce base
instructions: Heat a heavy pot over medium heat. Add 1 tbsp butter + remaining 1 tbsp oil.
Add onion and cook 6–8 minutes until soft and lightly golden.
Add crushed tomatoes. Simmer 12–18 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce darkens and thickens.
checks: Cook down tomatoes to remove raw flavor.
If splattering, reduce heat and partially cover.

steps.title: Blend (and optionally strain) for a silky sauce
instructions: Turn off heat and cool 3–5 minutes.
Blend sauce until very smooth (blender or immersion blender).
Optional: strain through a fine mesh strainer for restaurant-smooth texture.
checks: Do not blend boiling hot liquid in a sealed blender—vent lid and start low.
Straining is optional but gives the signature silky finish.

steps.title: Finish the sauce with butter, cream, and kasuri methi
instructions: Return smooth sauce to the pot over low heat.
Stir in remaining butter (3 tbsp) until melted.
Add cream and stir gently; keep heat low to avoid splitting.
Add garam masala and crushed kasuri methi; simmer 3–5 minutes.
Taste and adjust salt (use remaining salt as needed). Optional: tiny squeeze of lemon for brightness.
checks: Keep heat low after adding cream.
Kasuri methi is powerful—start with 2 tsp, adjust to taste.

steps.title: Combine chicken and serve
instructions: Add charred chicken (and any resting juices) to the sauce.
Simmer 5–8 minutes on low until chicken is warmed through and coated.
Serve with basmati rice or naan. Optional garnish: cilantro and a drizzle of cream.
checks: Avoid boiling—gentle heat keeps the sauce silky.
If sauce thickens too much, thin with a splash of water.

notes: Cool quickly; store covered.
Reheat gently on low; avoid boiling to prevent sauce splitting.
Freeze in portions. Thaw overnight in fridge.
Reheat gently; add a small splash of cream after reheating if needed.

weeknightNotes: Use broiler or air fryer for fast char.
Sauce can be made ahead; chicken can be batch-charred and frozen.
batchNotes: Sauce freezes well; kasuri methi is best added after reheating for maximum aroma.
Double batch the sauce base for future quick curries.

notes: Cook chicken to 165°F / 74°C.
Avoid boiling after adding cream/yogurt-based elements (prevents splitting/curdling).
Cool leftovers within 2 hours; reheat until steaming hot.

add.notes: Soak 10 minutes in hot water; blend into sauce.

notes: Blend soaked cashews into the sauce before straining for extra body.
Adjust salt and add a touch more acid to balance.
Use unsweetened coconut yogurt and coconut cream.
Finish with extra lemon to brighten and counter coconut sweetness.
Add 1/2–1 tsp hot chili powder or a small minced serrano to the sauce base.
Keep Kashmiri for color; add heat separately.
